mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which compounds corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects supply-side bottlenecks and fading economic momentum, causing equity valuations to contract over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases produce positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent annualized confirms robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDPC1 above 2.0 percent yields negative 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of post-release windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading above 3.5 percent forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which elevates discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 3.5 percent is followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts household income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Concurrent PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline yields negative 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expansion during a monetary easing cycle signals that accommodative policy successfully transmits to real economic activity, generating immediate positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth during easing cycles produces negative 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of observations.
